title: lead-u16 reconciliation BLOCKED: shopsystem-docs BC emitted work_done with dirty tree (post-cw7-fix non-adoption)
description:
lead-u16 work_done arrived in lead inbox claiming canonical .claude/settings.json re-pour from shopsystem-templates@7ec5e75. Empirical verification on /workspaces/shopsystem-product/repos/shopsystem-docs:

  - Re-pour commit DID land: bfb52cb 'chore(lead-u16): re-pour .claude/settings.json from canonical bc.json' on main (1 commit ahead of origin/main — parent will push).
  - .claude/settings.json is byte-identical to canonical: 'bd prime' + bare 'shop-msg prime' (no --bc flag, no {{SHOP_NAME}} placeholder). Acceptance met for the deliverable.

HOWEVER the BC's working tree was DIRTY at the time of emit:
  - Modified (uncommitted): .claude/agents/bc-reviewer.md (+137 lines — appears to be a partial re-pour or local edit)
  - Untracked: .specstory/, features/, pyproject.toml, src/, tests/, walkthrough/

Per lead-cw7 (now CLOSED, fix landed in shopsystem-templates commit 3f74c52), the canonical bc-reviewer.md template SHOULD refuse to emit work_done when the working tree is dirty. The fact that shopsystem-docs emitted anyway means this BC has NOT picked up the new canonical bc-reviewer.md template — distinct from the original cw7 pattern (which was about absence of the precondition entirely).

RECONCILIATION DECISION: lead-u16 work_done IS the canonical fix on settings.json and the commit IS on the BC's main, so the core deliverable is met. But the dirty-emit pattern is a non-adoption finding for shopsystem-docs that needs to be surfaced. The +137-line modification to bc-reviewer.md is itself the canonical template re-pour the BC needs to pick up — possibly mid-edit. Recommended next action: separately dispatch the canonical bc-reviewer.md re-pour to shopsystem-docs (similar to the lead-u16 settings re-pour), and have the BC commit-or-revert its uncommitted bc-reviewer.md change.

Filed during lead-u16 reconciliation 2026-05-28.
close_reason: misframed — root cause is not cw7 non-adoption but cw7-only-covered-Reviewer-path. The Reviewer is not in the loop for request_maintenance work (only assign_scenarios); the dirty-tree emit on lead-u16 / lead-jch goes through the Implementer, whose work_done precondition is unfixed. Tracked under lead-8lm (Implementer-template symmetric coverage).

title: mechanism: shop-msg respond collision is unrecoverable — consume outbox does not free lead-inbox row for re-emit
description:
From mechanism_observation lead-cw7-reviewer-recovery (observed_during lead-cw7). The shopsystem-messaging BC's insert_bc_response collision-checks on the LEAD-INBOX row, while consume_outbox_message only flips consumed=TRUE on the BC-side OUTBOX marker (storage.py:444-466 vs 846-860). A BC that emits a degraded or test work_done (or wrong message_type) cannot amend or replace it; the only recovery is direct DB manipulation, which the BC template forbids. This is a one-shot trap that makes role discipline brittle in adversarial review.

CONCRETE EVIDENCE: lead-cw7 work_done payload landed with summary='test' and only 2/4 scenario_hashes during reviewer test invocation; the real complete response (95 hashes, full adversarial-probe trail, pre-emit verification evidence) was blocked by collision refusal. consume outbox did not free the slot. See lead-plt (the work_done emit-fidelity bead) for the downstream reconciliation problem this creates.

PROPOSED FIXES (any one; see lead-cw7-reviewer-recovery body for full rationale):
(a) Add --force to shop-msg respond {clarify,work_done,mechanism_observation} that DELETEs existing lead-inbox row before insert (gated; default safe).
(b) Make 'shop-msg consume outbox' also release the lead-inbox slot (single-source-of-truth: consumed responses are no longer authoritative; BC may re-emit).
(c) Add 'shop-msg respond amend' as a distinct verb.

ADDITIONALLY: bc-reviewer / bc-implementer templates should warn that ANY --status/--summary/--scenario-hash test invocation lands a permanent row.

Cross-links: lead-cw7 (parent), lead-plt (downstream emit-fidelity defect this enables).

title: [finding] verified-deposited lead-shop dispatch (lead-xea) lost from BC inbox between sessions
description:
FINDING — first observed case of a real, verified-deposited lead-shop dispatch disappearing from BC inbox state between sessions (not within a single session under LISTEN drop, and not a synthetic-fixture row).

TIMELINE:
- 2026-05-22: 'shop-msg send request_maintenance --bc shopsystem-devcontainer --work-id lead-xea' executed; deposit verified via 'shop-msg read inbox --bc shopsystem-devcontainer --work-id lead-xea' (see lead-xea NOTES).
- 2026-05-28: 'shop-msg pending inbox --bc shopsystem-devcontainer' returns empty; 'shop-msg read inbox --bc shopsystem-devcontainer --work-id lead-xea' returns 'no inbox message found'. lead-xea bead still in_progress P1. Devcontainer Dockerfile still lacks gh; .github/workflows/publish-devcontainer.yml still targets shopsystem-devcontainer (not shopsystem-bc-base); no ghcr.io/dstengle/shopsystem-bc-base image on the lead host. The BC never received and acted on the work — the message vanished.

DISTINCTION FROM ADJACENT BEADS:
- lead-tsj: in-session LISTEN connection drop + inbox/outbox state loss. Closest fit — same failure mode (verified-deposited rows disappear from postgres state) but the lead-xea instance crossed a session boundary, not an observable LISTEN drop within one session. It may be the same root cause (e.g., a destructive operation in the test suite or a non-transactional cleanup nuking real rows), or a second instance worth tracking.
- lead-497: BC test fixtures leak fxhyg-/test-bc-/test-lead- synthetic rows. Opposite surface (synthetic rows survive; here, real rows died).
- lead-vvz: shopsystem-messaging conftest synthetic-name fixtures lack finalizers. Same opposite surface as lead-497.

OBSERVATION VALUE:
This is the first known production-impacting instance of message loss (a real BC dispatch with downstream blockers — lead-7gt — was silently dropped, leaving the lead-shop bead and the BC repo state out of sync). Strengthens the lead-tsj root-cause case and motivates raising lead-tsj from P2 to P1 once correlated.

REMEDIATION ON THIS WORK:
lead-xea re-dispatched 2026-05-28; deposit re-verified. lead-xea bead notes updated. No further action on the work itself.

CROSS-LINK:
- Related: lead-tsj (closest fit, in-session variant), lead-497, lead-vvz, lead-xea (the re-dispatched work).
- See lead-xea NOTES for the timeline and verification commands.

title: mechanism: BC role template work_done precondition does not enforce 'committed to origin/main' before emit (3rd occurrence)
description:
REPEATING PATTERN observed across multiple BCs and round trips. The BC role template's work_done precondition checks that implementation is correct on the working tree but does NOT verify the change is committed (let alone pushed to origin/main) before allowing the BC to emit work_done. Result: Architect reconciliation discovers the work is on the working tree only, has to file a blocker bead, BC re-emits work_done after committing, lead has to re-reconcile. Multi-trip waste per dispatch.

OCCURRENCES TO DATE (2026-05-27 → 2026-05-28):
  1. lead-0bw / shopsystem-messaging — first observation, captured by lead-b6x (2026-05-27 18:53Z).
  2. lead-33r / shopsystem-templates — same-day repeat on different BC, captured by lead-qwt (2026-05-27 ~21Z). Now closed: BC pushed commit 7ec5e75 and re-reconciliation succeeded.
  3. lead-0bw / shopsystem-messaging — RE-FAILURE on the same bead 2026-05-28: BC re-emitted work_done a second time (visible in outbox), but HEAD on shopsystem-messaging is still b14b0ba (lead-e9x), no lead-0bw commit, conftest.py still modified +2211/-34 in working tree. lead-b6x remains the BC-specific blocker.

ROOT CAUSE HYPOTHESIS:
The BC role templates (bc-implementer.md / bc-reviewer.md in shopsystem-templates) instruct the BC to verify scenario hashes, run BDD, summarize work — but do not list 'git log shows the lead-<id> commit on origin/main HEAD' as a precondition for emitting work_done via shop-msg respond work_done. The shop primer's session-close discipline ('Work is not done until git push succeeds') applies to lead-shop work but is not surfaced as a template-enforced check on the BC side.

CANDIDATE FIXES (PO/Architect to refine):
  (a) Add an explicit precondition to the BC Implementer template: 'Before composing work_done, run bb866b9 chore(bd): resubmit lead-33r; reopen lead-0bw; file lead-b6x and lead-9r4 and verify the lead-<id> commit is the latest (or present). If not, you have not finished work — commit and push first.'
  (b) Have shop-msg respond work_done check the BC's git state and refuse to send if HEAD is dirty or origin/main lacks an expected work_id-tagged commit. Heavier hammer; messaging BC must own the check.
  (c) Add a Reviewer-template gate: Reviewer refuses to approve work_done emit until the commit is on origin/main.

CROSS-LINKS:
  - lead-b6x (lead-0bw blocker, OPEN) — keep open; note today's re-failure.
  - lead-qwt (lead-33r blocker, CLOSED) — pattern captured.
  - This bead names the cross-BC pattern that lead-b6x and lead-qwt each captured as a single instance.

POSITIVE TEST FOR FIX: After role-template change, dispatch a fresh work item to a BC. Have the BC reach work_done emission without committing. The BC's tooling/template/Reviewer gate refuses the emit and surfaces 'commit + push first' as the required action.
notes: 2026-05-28 OCCURRENCE #5 (4th distinct BC): lead-jch / shopsystem-devcontainer emitted work_done with worktree-dirty and zero commits for lead-jch. HEAD = 6542ac4 (different work_id). All four files in worktree only; workflow on main still publishes old image name; ghcr image not published. Notably, the BC's work_done summary explicitly states it considers commit-and-push the lead's job — meaning canonical bc-implementer template adoption is still incomplete on shopsystem-devcontainer (parallels lead-u16 / shopsystem-docs which had not adopted the bc-reviewer commit-on-origin/main gate). lead-jch reconciliation BLOCKED; lead-7gt remains blocked transitively. See lead-jch notes for full evidence.

title: BDD suite uses ephemeral postgres instance (not production shopsystem DB)
description:
Sibling to lead-6nt (registry surface). Today's Monitor event lead-dual-1 work_done from shopsystem-messaging revealed that BDD step defs in repos/shopsystem-messaging/features/ invoke shop-msg respond using the canonical bc=shopsystem-messaging name (not the per-test tmp_path namespace), so the respond payload writes to the production lead inbox and broadcasts via LISTEN/NOTIFY.

POLLUTION CONFIRMED (lead inbox 2026-05-27): 16 orphan rows for synthetic work_ids — lead-dual-1, lead-901/902/903, lead-200, lead-301/302, lead-500/501/502, lead-col1/col2/col3, lead-001. None correspond to real beads. All map to feature files in repos/shopsystem-messaging/features/ (consume_outbox, cwd_implicit_shop_resolution, pending_and_read_inbox, respond_*, send_request_maintenance*, watch).

WHY NOT FOLDED INTO LEAD-6NT
Scenarios 39-44 (queued under lead-6nt) constrain their assertions to shop_registry rows. The inbox/outbox surface is a different table with its own save/restore boundary. Critically, simply rewriting step defs to use tmp_path-namespaced bc args is NOT a valid fix: scenarios 25, 38, and others EXPLICITLY assert lead-inbox routing behavior with canonical names — that routing IS the contract under test.

CANDIDATE STRATEGIES (for PO to refine into scenarios):
  (a) Session-scoped save/restore of inbox/outbox rows for production canonical names the BDD suite touches (parallel to scenario 43's design for shop_registry).
  (b) Work_id prefix discipline (e.g., reserve a 'test-' prefix that the lead's pending views filter; rewrite fixture work_ids to use it).
  (c) Dedicated test-scope SHOPMSG_DSN distinct from production (most isolating; biggest refactor).
  (d) Per-test cleanup: at function-scoped teardown, delete inbox/outbox rows the test created via shop-msg respond/send (requires identifying them — possibly via a tag, possibly by snapshot/diff).

POSITIVE TEST FOR THE FIX: After a BDD session completes, shop-msg pending inbox --lead shopsystem-product returns no row whose work_id is not present in 'bd list' (i.e., is not a real bead).

ATTEMPTED CLEANUP TODAY
shop-msg consume outbox --bc shopsystem-messaging --work-id lead-dual-1 --message-type work_done failed because the canonical name shopsystem-messaging currently resolves via shop_registry to a pytest-212 tmp_path — i.e., lead-6nt's registry corruption is also active. The two beads share the same root cause class but address distinct surfaces; lead-6nt repairs the registry, this bead drains the inbox/outbox.

OUT OF SCOPE
  - Editing repos/shopsystem-messaging directly (lead shop is hands-off code).
  - Dispatching a request_maintenance now — PO should first decide the strategy, then author scenarios to be dispatched under a normal lead-architect vehicle.

title: mechanism observation (lead-i18): test fixtures register production canonical names without save/restore
description:
BC shopsystem-messaging flagged in lead-i18 work_done summary that the test-suite step helper _register_shop (tests/conftest.py:3975) calls registry_add() against the postgres shop_registry using production canonical names (e.g., 'shopsystem-docs', 'unregistered-bc', 'shopsystem-product') derived from feature-file Given steps. The session-scoped fixture only saves/restores 'shopsystem-product' and 'shopsystem product'; it does NOT save/restore 'shopsystem-docs' or any other production BC name a future scenario may use.

LOAD-BEARING: The cwd_implicit_shop_resolution feature (just landed via lead-i18) registers 'shopsystem-docs' pointing at a tmp_path during the test run. If the test session crashes between registry_add and registry_remove, or if the cleanup path is bypassed, the production registry row for 'shopsystem-docs' is left pointing at a defunct pytest tmp_path. CWD-implicit shop-msg invocations from the real repos/shopsystem-docs/ working tree would then resolve via the corrupted registry entry. The mechanism (CWD-implicit resolution) is the very thing whose hygiene this fixture pattern threatens.

VERIFICATION ANCHORS (in repos/shopsystem-messaging):
- tests/conftest.py:3975 _register_shop -- direct registry_add, no save
- tests/conftest.py:157-174 _ensure_session_lead -- only 'shopsystem-product' / 'shopsystem product' saved
- features/cwd_implicit_shop_resolution.feature lines 6, 16, 54, 75 -- production canonical names appearing in Given steps

RECOMMENDED FOLLOW-UP (defer dispatch):
1. Extend _SAVED_PRODUCTION_ENTRIES initialization to cover every canonical name that step defs may pass to _register_shop, OR
2. Refactor _register_shop to use disambiguated test-only names (uuid suffix) when the scenario does not specifically require a production name, OR
3. Add a per-test cleanup (autouse function-scoped fixture) that snapshots & restores every name _register_shop touches during that test.

Likely vehicle: request_maintenance (flat refactor of test hygiene, no new scenarios) to shopsystem-messaging. Defer until either (a) a related scenario lands that would amplify the risk, or (b) an actual corruption event occurs.

notes:

OBSERVED STATE (BC repo /workspaces/shopsystem-product/repos/shopsystem-docs):
  - features/ contains exactly ONE file: adoption_walkthrough_terminal_section.feature, pinning 56babfaeec4514a3 only.
  - grep '@scenario_hash' in BC features/ returns only 56babfaeec4514a3.
  - BC work_done echoes the 4 other hashes, but they are not in the BC's features/ tree.
  - lead-km9 has no recorded work_done in the BC outbox (shop-msg read outbox for lead-km9 returns only the BC's clarify).

INTERPRETATION: per ADR-009 vehicle (b), the BC was expected to emit ONE consolidated work_done covering 56babfaeec4514a3 (the lead-b34 replacement) plus the four unchanged originals — addressed to the lead-b34 work_id. The BC did echo all five hashes in scenario_hashes:, but in fact only landed the replacement; the four others were not implemented. The work_done's hash list misrepresents BC state.

POSSIBLE CAUSES:
  1. BC understood the dispatch as ONLY covering the replacement (lead-b34 description framed it as additive — 'carries only the revised scenario'). BC may have inferred that the other four would be tracked via a separate work_done on lead-km9, and is still in flight on those.
  2. BC mechanically copied the hash list from the lead-b34 dispatch payload into work_done.scenario_hashes without verifying each hash had a corresponding feature file. (ADR-009 v2 candidate: tighten the BC-side requirement that scenario_hashes in work_done must be observably pinned in features/.)

FOLLOW-UP OPTIONS:
  (a) Send request_shop_card to shopsystem-docs to read its authoritative scenario register — confirms what BC believes it has pinned.
  (b) If BC is genuinely still in flight on the four originals, leave lead-km9 open and wait.
  (c) If BC considers lead-km9 closed (because lead-b34 work_done echoed all hashes), the four originals need to be re-dispatched. Architect to decide vehicle (likely a fresh request_bugfix or assign_scenarios depending on whether BC has any partial work).

ALSO: BC's working tree has uncommitted untracked files (features/, src/, tests/, walkthrough/) — orthogonal hygiene concern.

ADR-009 v2 FRICTION: this is the first BC work_done landing against the canonized vehicle-(b) workaround. The friction point: the BC's work_done.scenario_hashes echoed the entire original dispatch's hash list when only one was actually pinned. The pattern as canonized does not unambiguously tell the BC which hashes it must observably pin to declare work_done — particularly when the bugfix dispatch is additive vs. replacement. Candidate for ADR-009 v2: spell out the BC's work_done scenario_hashes contract explicitly (must be a strict subset of BC's pinned @scenario_hash set in features/), and clarify whether work_done on a clarify-resolution bead is allowed to claim coverage of unchanged sibling hashes from the original dispatch.

Superseded by lead-eqx (re-dispatch of the four unpinned scenarios via assign_scenarios, dispatched 2026-05-27) and ADR-010 (canonizes the work_done.scenario_hashes strict-subset rule that prevents the lead-b34 echo pattern in future).

title: Lead subagent templates missing Claude Code YAML frontmatter
description:
The canonical templates for lead-po.md and lead-architect.md (in repos/shopsystem-templates/src/shop_templates/templates/) lack the YAML frontmatter (---\nname: ...\ndescription: ...\n[tools: ...]\n---) that Claude Code requires to register a markdown file as an invokable subagent. As a result, when shop-templates bootstrap pours a lead shop, the resulting .claude/agents/lead-po.md and .claude/agents/lead-architect.md are present on disk but unregistered: the runtime reports 'Agent type "lead-po" not found' (and likewise for lead-architect) when the router attempts to dispatch.

Empirical demonstration: from /workspaces/shopsystem-product, an Agent invocation with subagent_type=lead-po failed with the registration error above; the available-agents list contained only the runtime defaults (claude-code-guide, Explore, general-purpose, Plan, statusline-setup) and none of the lead subagents.

This blocks the entire lead-shop role-isolation pattern documented in lead-primer.md §'Who you are — router for lead-po and lead-architect subagents.' The router cannot delegate PO or architect work, which means briefs/PDRs/scenarios cannot be PO-authored and request_bugfix/assign_scenarios/request_maintenance dispatches cannot be architect-composed.

The BC subagent templates in the same directory (bc-implementer.md, bc-reviewer.md) DO carry the required frontmatter — they are the pattern to match. The fix is to add an analogous frontmatter block to lead-po.md and lead-architect.md whose name/description fields accurately describe each lead-shop role's invocation criteria.

This bug was likely introduced in commit 88ca83c ('agents: lead-po and lead-architect subagents (PDR-002 path a)').

NOTE on dispatch protocol: this request_bugfix is being filed router-direct rather than architect-composed because the architect subagent is itself one of the unreachable subagents — composing the dispatch through the proper vehicle requires the very capability this bugfix restores. Router-direct is the one-off needed to bootstrap the architect role itself; future dispatches return to architect-composed once shopsystem-templates ships the fix and we re-pour.
notes: Reconciled 2026-05-27: shopsystem-templates work_done verified. .claude/agents/lead-po.md and .claude/agents/lead-architect.md frontmatter (name/description/tools) confirmed well-formed and matching the canonical templates at repos/shopsystem-templates/src/shop_templates/templates/lead-{po,architect}.md. Re-pour already applied locally via commit 15037cf. No further re-pour to other shops needed (sibling BC repos don't use lead subagents). Outbox row already consumed. No scenarios, no register check; bug fix is doc-shape only.
close_reason: Closed

title: [bug] request_bugfix: messaging test suite clobbers production registry on every run
description: The shopsystem-messaging pytest suite overwrites production shop_registry entries on every run. In tests/conftest.py the session-scoped _ensure_session_lead fixture (lines 87-114) calls registry_add('shopsystem-product', str(root.resolve()), shop_type='lead') and registry_add('shopsystem product', str(root.resolve()), shop_type='lead') with a tmp_path as the shop_root. registry_add uses ON CONFLICT ... DO UPDATE (storage.py lines 630-640), so these calls overwrite existing production entries pointing to /workspaces/shopsystem-product. After any test run, shop-msg registry list shows 'shopsystem product' and 'shopsystem-product' pointing at /tmp/pytest-of-vscode/pytest-NNN/... paths. The teardown at line 128 only removes the random test-lead-session-* entry; the two named entries are never cleaned up. Similarly, the step-def at line 3439 (given_bc_registered) registers any bc_name passed in from a feature file under a tmp_path with no teardown. Operational effect: shop-msg pending outbox --lead 'shopsystem product' silently returns empty; resolve_lead_shop() routes BC responses to a tmp path. Recovery requires manually re-running shop-msg registry add after every test run. Fix: the test suite must not upsert well-known production names. Either (a) use a separate database or schema for tests (SHOPMSG_DSN already supports this via env var), (b) save and restore the original registry values around the session fixture, or (c) add a finalizer that removes the two named entries and re-inserts the original values. Source: tests/conftest.py lines 87-128 and 3439-3448; storage.py registry_add lines 622-642.
notes: Reconciled 2026-05-27: shopsystem-messaging work_done verified. Pre-existing 48 failures (subprocess PATH gap, unrelated). Probes all green. title: [bug] request_bugfix: shop-msg pending inbox --bc returns empty despite unconsumed rows
description: shop-msg pending inbox --bc <name> resolves the BC path via resolve_shop_name() (storage.py:732-747) and then runs query_pending_inbox(bc_root) (storage.py:277-302). That query filters by bc = <resolved_path>. When the registry entry for the BC name has been overwritten by test fixtures (Bug D), resolve_shop_name() returns a tmp path and the query finds zero matching rows even though the real inbox row exists under the production path. Additionally, query_pending_inbox uses NOT EXISTS (outbox row) as the pending predicate, but the messages table also has a consumed column; if consumed semantics are used on the inbox side in future this predicate diverges. Observed: shop-msg pending inbox --bc shopsystem-bc-launcher returned empty while DB row (id=7, bc=/workspaces/shopsystem-product/repos/shopsystem-bc-launcher, work_id=lead-1wv, direction=inbox, consumed=False) existed. Note: lead-yaj covers only the outbox direction (pending outbox --lead filtering); this is the separate inbox direction bug. Source: src/shop_msg/storage.py lines 277-302 and src/shop_msg/cli.py lines 168-182 (_resolve_bc) and 585-596 (_cmd_pending_inbox).
close_reason: Closed

title: [bug] request_bugfix: shop-msg pending outbox --lead returns empty despite unconsumed rows in DB
description: shop-msg pending outbox --lead <name> calls query_pending_outbox(lead_root) in storage.py lines 305-361. The query fetches ALL outbox rows with consumed=FALSE (lines 338-346), then Python-filters for rows whose bc column starts with lead_root + '/repos/' (lines 356-359). The filter fails when the BC's registered shop_root in the registry does not start with that prefix — for example, when insert_bc_response writes the BC-side outbox marker using the path stored in the registry, and that path is the canonical filesystem path to the BC clone. If the path stored in the registry differs from <lead_root>/repos/<bc-name> (e.g., an absolute path outside that tree), all rows are filtered out silently. Observed: three unconsumed rows (lead-80i, lead-9d3, lead-dzv) were present in the DB with consumed=False; shop-msg read outbox --bc <name> --work-id <id> read them correctly; shop-msg pending outbox --lead returned empty. Fix: query_pending_outbox must either (a) join against shop_registry to enumerate BCs registered under the lead, or (b) use the registry path to correctly scope the bc-column filter.

comments.text: Reconciliation observation 2026-05-22: Monitor stream at session start flushed 'lead-1t2 clarify' but 'shop-msg pending outbox --lead' did not list it. Investigation: lead-1t2 is in 'pending inbox --lead' (a BC->lead clarify routed to lead inbox), NOT in 'pending outbox --lead' (which tracks BC outbox rows). The two commands surface different message-flow directions, which is consistent with the data model — but the router's mental model 'lead-1t2 should be in pending outbox' suggests a follow-up bead may be warranted: a unified 'pending --lead' that merges inbox-clarifies-from-bcs and outbox-responses-from-bcs into one list, so the operator does not have to remember to run two commands. title: [bug] request_bugfix: shop-msg watch silently continues on LISTEN connection drop without exiting
description: shop-msg watch --lead holds a psycopg3 autocommit connection and blocks in conn.notifies() (storage.py:watch_lead_inbox lines 1013-1029 and watch_inbox lines 1135-1156). If the TCP connection to Postgres drops mid-session, psycopg3 does not raise immediately — the notifies() generator silently starves with no output and no exit. There is no exception handler around the LISTEN loop, no timeout, no keepalive-expiry detection, and no reconnection logic. The watcher appears running (process alive, no stderr) but delivers no notifications. Recovery requires manual monitor restart. Fix: detect connection drop (OperationalError or generator exhaustion) and either exit non-zero with a clear stderr message, or attempt bounded reconnection with per-attempt stdout logging.

title: request_bugfix: brief 003 implementation (12 scenarios 57-68, post-supersession re-dispatch)
description:
Re-dispatch of brief 003's 12 scenarios (57-68) to shopsystem-templates, carried as request_bugfix per BC's earlier vehicle observation (BC already has bootstrap capability; brief 003 extends it).

BACKGROUND
==========
The lead-xkh chain hit a coordination gap. Sequence:

- lead-xkh (assign_scenarios, 12 scenarios 57-68 for brief 003: event-driven shop activation, canonical .claude/settings.json, bootstrap extension) → BC clarified on contradiction with prior-pinned scenario 51 (compound AGENTS.md+settings.json pin in bootstrap_bd_init_side_effects.feature).

But: lead-5mz carried ONLY scenario 69. Brief 003's 12 positive pins (.claude/settings.json writing, prereqs naming, venv-sync, etc.) were never delivered to the BC. lead-xkh still has a clarify in its outbox, so the BC's router will not re-process it. To get brief 003 implemented, the lead must send a NEW dispatch carrying the 12 brief 003 scenarios.

This is a protocol gap shape: the §4.4 closure assumption that lead-5mz's terminal work_done closes lead-xkh's chain doesn't hold because lead-5mz only carried the supersession scenario, not the brief 003 positive pins. Prior chains (lead-bwu→lead-e4g→lead-14e) worked because each follow-up bundled the prior scenarios. The lead-5mz dispatch instruction was wrong on this — it should have bundled.

LINKAGE
=======
- Predecessors: lead-xkh (assign_scenarios for brief 003, clarify-stalled in BC outbox), lead-5mz (closed; clean supersession of scenario 51).
- lead-xkh remains in_progress with a pointer to this new bead. lead-xkh closes implicitly when this new work_id's terminal work_done arrives in the BC outbox.

SUPERSESSION STATE
==================
- Prior scenario 51 (hash 7d64bb8ed5a3f656, compound AGENTS.md+settings.json pin) is retired. Its settings.json clause was removed by lead-5mz.
- New scenario 69 (hash 1a6e90189f9c2ade, AGENTS.md-only outcome pin) lives in BC features/bootstrap_no_top_level_agents_md.feature.
- Brief 003's scenario 60 (bootstrap writes canonical .claude/settings.json per shop type) no longer conflicts with anything in the BC — the contradiction lead-xkh tripped on is resolved.

PAYLOAD (12 scenarios 57-68)
============================
Implements brief 003 items A–E:

- A (canonical .claude/settings.json templates ship from shop-templates): scenarios 57, 68.
- B (SessionStart inotifywait hook, four reference-realization properties): scenarios 58, 65.
- C (lead-shop settings.json composes with existing bd prime hook): scenario 59.

  features/templates/64-canonical-claude-md-primer-names-activation-host-prereqs.gherkin
  features/templates/65-canonical-sessionstart-fails-loudly-on-missing-inotifywait.gherkin
  features/templates/66-bootstrapped-shop-resolves-bc-cli-invocations-to-live-source-on-session-start.gherkin
  features/templates/67-lead-bootstrap-installs-sibling-bc-clones-editable-into-product-venv.gherkin
  features/templates/68-canonical-bc-settings-json-does-not-name-lead-watch-target.gherkin

IMPLEMENTATION NOTES (previously prepared, surfaced here since assign_scenarios was retired in favour of request_bugfix and request_bugfix's description IS the carrier for procedural framing)
=========================================================================================================================================================================================

- Scope item E realization framing: scenario 66 is the outcome pin (BC CLI invocations resolve to live source under repos/ on session start). Scenario 67 is the mechanism pin (lead-bootstrap installs sibling BC clones editable into product venv). The two compose: 67 is one valid realization of 66, but 66 is the durable contract the Reviewer attests against, and 67 names the specific mechanism the canonical templates encode today. If a future realization replaces editable installs with a different live-source resolution (e.g., a path-aware shim), 66 remains intact and 67 gets superseded.

- Scenario 65 (loud-fail on missing inotifywait): the canonical SessionStart hook MUST produce a visible failure when inotifywait is missing on PATH; silently degrading to a no-watcher state is the failure mode this scenario pins against. The canonical settings.json should encode this property via the hook's startup command shape (e.g., exit-on-missing-binary), not via a separate runtime probe.

- Scenario 59 (lead settings.json composition): the lead-shop's canonical settings.json composes both the inotifywait activation hook AND the bd prime hook on SessionStart. Concretely the SessionStart entry has multiple hook commands, not a single replaced command. This is so the activation hook does NOT clobber bd prime (operational hygiene).

title: scenarios 57-68: event-driven shop activation, canonical .claude/settings.json + bootstrap extension (brief 003)
description:
Brief 003 (lead-0h1) — Event-driven shop activation. 12 scenarios extending the
shop-templates bootstrap surface to ship a canonical `.claude/settings.json`
per shop type plus host-prereq naming, and to install sibling BC clones
editable from the lead bootstrap.

The brief carries two invariants and five scope items:

- Invariant 1: every shop is reactive on session start (BC watches own
  `inbox/`; lead watches `repos/*/outbox/`).
- Invariant 2: activation honors brief 001's encapsulation — the
  watcher fires a wake signal; reading content still goes through
  `shop-msg`.
- Scope A: canonical `.claude/settings.json` per shop type (scenarios 57, 68).
- Scope B: SessionStart hook content with stdbuf-wrapped inotifywait,
  alias-bypassed flushing filter, merged 2>&1 for startup-chatter
  visibility, monitor-mode persistent arming (scenarios 58, 59, 65).
- Scope C: bootstrap writes settings.json; update re-pours it as a
  bootstrap-managed file (scenarios 60, 61, 62).
- Scope D: prereq naming (`inotify-tools`, `coreutils`, `inotifywait`,
  `stdbuf`) in bootstrap-generated `CLAUDE.md` and the canonical primer
  (scenarios 63, 64).
- Scope E: live-source resolution outcome + lead bootstrap installs
  sibling BC clones editable into the product venv (scenarios 66, 67).

## Empirically validated context

This session validated the stdbuf-wrapped inotifywait pipeline end-to-end
on lead-79q reconciliation: sub-second event-fire on `work_done`
arrival via the reference pipeline named in scope item B. The pattern
works. Scope items A/B/C/D codify the canonical shape so every
bootstrapped shop gets it by default.

## Pre-state cited

Pre-state verification on shopsystem-templates current state:

- Package data at `src/shop_templates/templates/`: 4 role-prompt files
  (`bc-implementer.md`, `bc-reviewer.md`, `lead-po.md`, `lead-architect.md`)
  + 2 CLAUDE.md primers under `templates/claude/` (`bc.md`, `lead.md`) +
  `gitignore.template`. No `.claude/settings.json` template exists.
- `src/shop_templates/cli.py`: bootstrap writes `.claude/agents/*.md`,
  top-level `CLAUDE.md` (from primer + `{{SHOP_NAME}}` substitution),
  top-level `.gitignore`, and invokes `bd init --skip-agents`. No
  `.claude/settings.json` is written. `update` re-pours managed
  agent files only — no settings.json handling.
- Empirical bootstrap of a probe directory
  (`shop-templates bootstrap --shop-type bc --shop-name probe
  --target /tmp/probe-003`) produced exactly: `CLAUDE.md`,
  `.claude/agents/bc-implementer.md`, `.claude/agents/bc-reviewer.md`.
  No `.claude/settings.json`. Exit 0.
- `repos/shopsystem-templates/features/` scan: no existing scenarios
  pin SessionStart hooks, settings.json content, Monitor harness,
  inotifywait, or venv install mode.

All 12 scenarios target net-new capability — `assign_scenarios` is the
correct vehicle.

## Implementation notes (informational; bodies are the contract)

- Scope item E is realization-pinned by the PO via two scenarios:
  scenario 66 pins the outcome (live-source resolution on session
  start) and scenario 67 pins the install-shape (lead bootstrap
  installs sibling clones editable into the product venv). Scenario 67
  alone is the PO's leaning shape; the Implementer is free to add a
  hook-time refresh on top if pre-state on E surfaces drift cases not
  covered by 67 alone, or to treat 67 as sufficient.

- Scenario 65 pins the "loud fail on missing `inotifywait`" property.
  The Implementer must select a hook shape such that a host with no
  `inotifywait` on PATH surfaces a visible diagnostic — not a silent
  no-watcher state. A polling fallback that silently activates
  violates the invariant; if the Implementer commits a fallback, it
  must itself be a visible notification.

- Scenario 59 specifies the canonical lead variant **composes** with the
  existing `bd prime` `SessionStart` hook — two distinct entries under
  `SessionStart`, not a replacement.

- Scope D's prereq naming surface is the Implementer's call between
  `CLAUDE.md` primer (named in scenarios 63 + 64 as the realization)
  vs other places. The scenarios commit primer + bootstrap-generated
  CLAUDE.md as the surface; the Implementer must satisfy both.

## Procedural reminders

- Hash discipline: per `lead-wgv`, verify computed hashes against
  wire form, not against the body source pre-CLI. The `shop-msg send`
  CLI computes hashes from canonicalized wire form; the Implementer's

- Mailbox encapsulation: `shop-msg` is the only sanctioned mailbox

  do not bypass the CLI (per `lead-xq0`).

title: mechanism observation: postgres-backed shop-msg state lost across LISTEN connection drop
description:
Mechanism observation logged 2026-05-27.

SYMPTOM
'shop-msg watch --lead shopsystem-product' (in-session Monitor watcher armed
at session start) died with:

  psycopg.OperationalError: server closed the connection unexpectedly
      This probably means the server terminated abnormally
      before or while processing the request.

The last event streamed before the drop was 'lead-eqx work_done'. On
operator-initiated restart of the watcher the CLI surfaced the next layer
of damage: the messaging registry was empty (7 expected entries: 2 lead
aliases — 'shopsystem-product' and 'shopsystem product' — plus 5 BCs:
shopsystem-messaging, shopsystem-templates, shopsystem-bc-launcher,
shopsystem-devcontainer, shopsystem-docs). Inbox and outbox state were
also lost: 'shop-msg pending inbox --lead "shopsystem product"' and
'shop-msg pending outbox --lead "shopsystem product"' both returned
empty, including in-flight rows that had not yet been consumed
(lead-i18 assign_scenarios and lead-bhp request_bugfix, both addressed
to shopsystem-messaging, both verified deposited earlier in the same
session via 'shop-msg read inbox').

The 7 entries were manually re-registered and the two in-flight
dispatches were re-emitted (see lead-i18 and lead-bhp notes for
wire-hash equivalence to originals).

SUSPECTED ROOT CAUSE
Postgres instance backing shop-msg lost its volume / restarted without
persistent storage. Two candidate causes:

  (a) Postgres container restart without a persistent volume mount.
      Whatever orchestration brings the messages DB up may be using
      an ephemeral / tmpfs-backed data dir, so any restart wipes
      state.

  (b) Devcontainer attached to a *different* postgres instance than
      the one it had been using. Commit 4949706 ('chore: attach lead
      devcontainer to shopsystem docker network') changed the
      devcontainer's network topology recently and may have
      inadvertently shifted DSN resolution to a different service.

To distinguish:
  - Inspect 'docker volume ls' / 'docker inspect' on the postgres
    service the lead devcontainer's SHOPMSG_DSN points to.
  - Check whether the bound volume survives container restart.
  - Compare against pre-4949706 behavior if possible.

DISTINCT FROM lead-7v1
lead-7v1 was the silent-continue watcher bug — that bug was about
the watcher NOT exiting on OperationalError and continuing to print
nothing. The fix to that bug landed and the watcher now correctly
exits on connection drop, which is what produced the observed exit
here. The new finding is about UNDERLYING DB DURABILITY, not the
watcher's exit semantics.

OBSERVATION-ONLY
Do not dispatch. File on the lead tracker so it surfaces in 'bd ready'
for follow-up triage. Likely follow-ups (not blockers):
  - Audit docker-compose / orchestration for the postgres volume mount.
  - If the volume IS persistent and was lost anyway, escalate to
    shopsystem-messaging as a request_bugfix (the BC ought to surface
    a clearer fail-fast / recovery message rather than silently
    re-bootstrapping into an empty registry).
  - If the volume is NOT persistent, file an infrastructure brief on
    the lead tracker to add one.
notes:
2026-05-27 RE-FRAMING: lead-6nt now strongly suggests the 'empty/lost registry' symptom in this bead was NOT a postgres durability problem. It was very likely the same fixture-leak corruption captured under lead-6nt — a BDD test session called `_register_shop` against production canonical names without save/restore, leaving the rows pointed at pytest tmp_paths and (depending on cleanup timing) potentially removing the lead rows entirely.

EVIDENCE FOR THE FIXTURE HYPOTHESIS (over the durability hypothesis):
  - lead-6nt directly observed active corruption today (2026-05-27) with the exact failure mode: production canonical names overwritten with tmp_path entries from pytest-200 test runs.
  - The 'manual re-registration of 7 entries' described in this bead matches exactly the 7-row repair lead-6nt completed today.
  - In-flight inbox/outbox rows for lead-i18 / lead-bhp that this bead reported as 'lost' would have been emitted into a bc=tmp_path row instead of the canonical path — so they'd still exist in the DB but would not surface in `pending` queries keyed by canonical name. Today's investigation found exactly that pattern: id=688, 690 outbox rows for lead-i18 are keyed at the dead pytest-199 tmp_path.

WHAT REMAINS UNCERTAIN:
  - The watcher exit with `psycopg.OperationalError: server closed the connection unexpectedly` was a real DB-side event. The fixture hypothesis explains the post-restart 'empty registry' but does NOT explain the connection drop itself. Possibilities: (a) genuinely independent postgres restart that coincided with a polluting test run, (b) shop-msg watch reconnect logic encountered the corrupted registry and surfaced confusing state, (c) a postgres-side maintenance event unrelated to either cause.

UPDATED PRIORITY: probably lower than P2. The 'durability' angle is now suspect; the actionable angle is the fixture hygiene captured in lead-6nt. Leaving open for now to track the watcher-exit observation separately if it recurs.

CROSS-LINK: see lead-6nt (P1 OPEN) for the active mechanism-observation and queued request_bugfix.
2026-05-27 ADDITIONAL EVIDENCE — fixture leak surfaces in inbox table too.

Monitor on the lead-shop watcher fired `lead-dual-1 work_done` from
shopsystem-messaging during a BDD test run. None of the polluting work_ids
(16 distinct rows in the lead inbox: lead-dual-1, lead-901/902/903, lead-200,
lead-301/302, lead-500/501/502, lead-col1/col2/col3, lead-001) are real beads;
all map to literal strings baked into BC feature files under
repos/shopsystem-messaging/features/.

Cleanup attempt via `shop-msg consume outbox --bc shopsystem-messaging
--work-id lead-dual-1 ...` failed because the canonical name now resolves
through shop_registry to /tmp/pytest-of-vscode/pytest-212/... — meaning the
exact registry corruption this bead originally documented was reproduced
again today, mid-session. The "empty registry after watcher restart" symptom
this bead originally captured is now firmly attributed to the BDD fixture
leak rather than to postgres durability.

NEW SIBLING BEAD: lead-0bw (P1) covers the inbox/outbox fixture surface
distinct from lead-6nt's registry surface; cross-linked.

The watcher-exit-on-OperationalError half of this bead remains an
independent open observation — no new evidence today.

The default DSN must use the compose service name as the hostname (e.g. postgresql://postgres:5432/shopsystem if the service is named postgres), not localhost or 127.0.0.1 (which only works with network_mode: host). title: Clarify protocol gap — clarify-resolution requires explicit re-dispatch carrying original scenarios
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-13 during lead-xkh→lead-5mz→lead-1pi chain. Sister concern to lead-rv9 (work_done blocked).

Pattern observed: BC clarifies on a dispatch (lead-xkh). Lead resolves the clarify by dispatching a follow-up (lead-5mz) carrying ONLY the resolution scenario (scenario 69). BC processes lead-5mz cleanly but does NOT re-process lead-xkh — because lead-xkh has a clarify in outbox, it's no longer 'pending' from BC's view. The original scenarios in lead-xkh remain unimplemented until the lead sends ANOTHER dispatch (lead-1pi) carrying them.

This is a coordination gap:
- The §4.4 closure narrative says 'terminal work_done implicitly closes the chain.'
- But that only works when the follow-up CARRIES the prior scenarios (as lead-bwu→lead-e4g→lead-14e did via accumulation).
- If the follow-up carries ONLY the resolution (e.g., a small supersession), the original scenarios are stranded.

Two design questions:
1. Should the BC's protocol re-process an inbox message after a clarify is responded to? (e.g., 'clarify in outbox does not finalize pending state; lead's next dispatch with matching work_id signals re-process')
2. Or should the lead-side protocol explicitly carry-forward prior unimplemented scenarios in every follow-up? (current de facto pattern, but easy to miss)
3. Or should we have a new message type 'clarify_resolved' (or similar) that signals 'process the originally-dispatched scenarios now'?

The current workaround (lead-1pi re-dispatches the 12 brief 003 scenarios + supersession context in description) works but produces wire-form hash drift (because the feature-title wrapping changes between dispatches) and isn't machine-readable for tracking which dispatch carries which scenarios.

Sister concerns:
- lead-rv9 (work_done blocked protocol gap)
- lead-725 (singleton-inbox presumption)
- lead-4wy (work_id pattern asymmetry)
- lead-yug (@supersedes tag convention)

All four-plus are accumulating in a 'catalog protocol gaps' cluster that would benefit from a coordinated brief revision once the test-harness or other priority work creates pressure to consolidate.

Concrete impact on lead-1pi: wire-form hashes for scenarios 57-68 differ from lead-xkh dispatch (e.g., 57 was d4b8aac19b34ea8c then; 1621b59b0ea8b20b now). Implementer's BDD attestation will use the new hashes. The brief 003 intent semantically equivalent.
notes: 2026-05-22: Pattern observed again. BC shopsystem-bc-launcher clarified on on-wire work_id shopsystem-templates-918 (bd: lead-gua). Lead resolved by composing a fresh request_bugfix on a separate work_id lead-bxl carrying only the new scenario (08-missing-claude-json-warns-and-proceeds). Confirmed empirically via 'shop-msg send --help' that the CLI exposes no clarify-resolution vehicle — only {request_maintenance, assign_scenarios, request_bugfix}. The original lead-gua / 918 scenarios remain in the BC's inbox as not-yet-implemented; the lead-bxl bugfix is additive, not a re-dispatch of 918's scenarios. Reinforces design question #3 in this bead's description: a native 'clarify_resolved' message type (or equivalent) would let the lead signal 'process the originally-dispatched scenarios now' without composing a parallel bugfix or re-paying the wire-form hash cost.

title: Product venv installs drift from BC source after BC work lands (no auto-reinstall)
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-13 during lead-79q dispatch.

The product venv at /workspaces/shopsystem-product/.venv/ has shop_msg installed as a regular package (not editable). When BCs update their source (lead-231.1 added 'pending' subcommand; lead-bwu/14e/ijr updated CLI behavior), the venv install does NOT update automatically. Lead-shop CLI invocations against shop-msg (and likely shop-templates similarly) see the OLDER installed surface, not the BC's live source.

Concrete impact today: shop-msg pending didn't exist in the venv install despite being present in BC source. Architect for lead-79q hit this and worked around by direct ls of inbox/outbox — a violation of brief 001 invariant 1 (encapsulation; CLI is sole messaging surface) forced by tooling staleness. Re-installed manually via 'pip install -e /workspaces/shopsystem-product/repos/shopsystem-messaging' which fixed it. shop-templates likely has the same issue (Architect for lead-79q also noted product venv shop-templates lacks bootstrap/update).

Two facets:
(a) Install mode: BCs should be installed editable (-e) so source changes flow without reinstall. Currently appears to be non-editable.
(b) Reinstall trigger: even if editable, fresh BC clones / version changes need a way to refresh. SessionStart hook in brief 003 could include 'pip install -e repos/*/' to keep the venv synced.

Adjacent / related:
- lead-zmi (shop-msg PATH ergonomics) — same theme, different facet.
- Brief 003 SessionStart hook design — should include venv-resync as part of activating each shop.

The 'ls workaround' the Architect used is a discipline concern: the lead-architect template's encapsulation guidance should be tightened to say 'if shop-msg pending fails, FIX THE TOOLING (reinstall/PATH/version) not bypass'. Worth a separate template-tightening bead if recurring.

title: bc-implementer template should require hash-recompute verification after @scenario_hash tag edits (drain of messaging-d64)
description:
Drain of messaging-d64 mechanism_observation from shopsystem-messaging, observed during lead-5cv.

Finding: bc-implementer canonical template does not require the Implementer to verify a @scenario_hash:<value> tag by piping the file through 'scenarios hash' before claiming the tag is correct. In lead-231.2 the Implementer wrote 4 fabricated hash values; Reviewer caught it but only via lead-side reconciliation cost (full §4.4 round-trip via lead-5cv).

BC's proposed action: Add a 'verify hash tags' step in the bc-implementer template's 'Doing the work' sections for assign_scenarios and request_bugfix (and an equivalent clause for request_maintenance when it touches scenario tags) requiring 'scenarios hash' recompute equality before terminal response.

Target BC: shopsystem-templates (the canonical bc-implementer template is the templates BC's product; messaging BC cannot self-fix).

Vehicle hint: request_bugfix (template-language tightening of unpinned existing behavior).

Natural fold-in with lead-6t3 (round-4 outlook for brief 001 — per-step CLI naming for WRITE-mailbox actions). Both items tighten the bc-implementer canonical template via additional procedural discipline; both could be carried by one brief revision that consolidates 46-49 + write-mailbox cases + hash-verify step into a coordinated dispatch. Worth considering together when the next brief revision is authored.

Also adjacent: lead-wgv (scenarios hash canonicalization ambiguity — raw vs wire form). Tightening hash-verify discipline in the template is load-bearing; ensuring the BC's bc-implementer knows WHICH form to verify against requires lead-wgv to be resolved first (or the discipline step to be explicit about it).

Related: lead-6t3 (round-4 outlook), lead-wgv (canonicalization), lead-5cv (original incident).

title: Round-4 outlook: per-step CLI naming for WRITE-mailbox actions (shop-msg respond clarify | work_done | mechanism_observation) across all four canonical templates
description:
Follow-on refinement that emerged from brief 001 items B+D §4.4 process (rounds 2-3 dispatch chain lead-bwu -> lead-e4g -> lead-14e closed; scenarios 46-49 landed pinning per-step CLI naming for READ-mailbox actions across all four canonical templates).

Same regression mode as scenarios 46-49: future edit could regress 'respond via shop-msg respond clarify' to bare 'send the response' and only template-wide scenarios would gate it.
- Same applies to lead-po '### Responding to a clarify via the shop-msg CLI' step 4.

Recommendation from lead-231 notes: defer to a deliberate brief revision rather than dispatch-by-dispatch. The pattern is structural — every per-mailbox-action step in every procedural subsection of every role template wants the same discipline. Cleanest pin is one Scenario Outline with columns for (template, subsection heading, mailbox action, target CLI substring). That can supersede 46/47/48/49 cleanly.

Vehicle when dispatched: likely request_bugfix (behavior exists in templates, no scenario pins it at per-step granularity — finding-5 territory). PO authors a Scenario Outline; Architect verifies BC pre-state empirically (read each respond-step in each template, confirm CLI is named) and dispatches.

Not blocking anything. Brief 001 (lead-231) is being closed as substantively complete; this is a separate follow-on concern.

title: scenarios hash canonicalization ambiguity — raw body vs wrapped+tagged form
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-12 during lead-e4g dispatch (request_bugfix closing lead-bwu §4.4 loop).

The Architect observed: 'scenarios hash < features/templates/46-*.gherkin' (against the raw scenario body) produces 06328160e83d2995, while the hash 'shop-msg send' persists on the wire (validated by 'shop-msg read') is e6bdf2f33bfae0d1. The CLI computes against a wrapped+sentinel-tagged form, not the raw body.

This is the Slice-16-shaped mismatch named in the architect's role contract anti-rationalization section. Same shape almost surfaced in lead-231.2 / lead-5cv (Implementer fabricated 4 @scenario_hash values; Reviewer caught via 'cat file | scenarios hash' which DID match the post-edit canonical) — but that case's reconciliation worked because both Implementer and Reviewer used raw-body hashing on already-tagged feature files, where the @scenario_hash tag had been hand-edited. The two-canonicalization-domains problem didn't bite because both parties stayed within one domain.

For lead-e4g and any future dispatch, the canonical question matters:
- The BC's ScenarioPayload.hash == compute_scenario_hash(gherkin) enforcement uses which canonicalization?
- The 'scenarios hash' CLI computes which form?
- Implementers verifying tags need to match against what the catalog enforces.

Three resolution shapes:
(a) One canonical form across both. 'scenarios hash' wraps its input the same way shop-msg does; tag values across feature files / inboxes / outboxes are byte-identical for identical scenario semantic content.
(b) Two named forms with clear scope. 'scenarios hash --raw' and 'scenarios hash --wire' (or similar); documented when each applies.
(c) Status quo + documentation. Names the two domains and where each is canonical; templates and procedural reminders cite the right form.

(a) is the cleanest invariant; (b) is most explicit; (c) is lowest-touch but most failure-prone.

Related: lead-4wy (work_id pattern asymmetry), lead-rv9 (work_done blocked protocol gap), lead-725 (singleton-inbox presumption) — same shape of latent protocol/canonicalization constraint surfaced by real dispatches.

Adjacent: lead-5cv's Implementer-defect-and-Reviewer-catch worked because both used raw-body hashing on already-tagged files (within one canonicalization domain). A future dispatch where one party uses the wire-form hash and the other uses raw-body would silently disagree.

Concrete impact for current work: lead-e4g's @scenario_hash tag in the BC inbox is the wire form. The BC's Implementer / Reviewer must validate against the wire form, not via raw-body 'scenarios hash'. Worth a procedural reminder in the dispatch description (already included by Architect for lead-e4g).

title: work_done(blocked) protocol gap — lead-side response shape unspecified
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-12 by lead-231.2's blocked work_done — the first natural occurrence of the work_done(blocked) branch from from-prototype-1.md finding 3 and §8 (documented in bc_reviewer_prompt.md but unexercised).

The protocol defines that the Reviewer can emit work_done(blocked) when the implementation has a gap (not a scenario gap, which would be clarify). What it does NOT define is the lead-side response. The Reviewer's report names what's wrong; what message does the lead send back to get it fixed?

Options considered for lead-231.2's specific case:
- request_bugfix: tightens unpinned behavior via scenarios. Awkward — the gap is a hash-value defect on the BC's internal feature files, not a missing scenario pin.
- request_maintenance: flat change (value-only update of 4 specific hashes). Best fit for this concrete case; chosen for lead-231.2 follow-up.
- clarify: for asking questions, not for 'redo your work'. Doesn't fit.
- No message; BC re-submits unilaterally: protocol doesn't authorize BC to re-emit work_done without lead input.

What needs designing:
1. Spec-level: does the existing 7-type catalog cover work_done(blocked) responses adequately by composition (e.g., 'always request_maintenance for value-only fixes the Reviewer specified'), or is a new mechanism needed?
2. Template-level: bc_reviewer prompt should constrain the work_done(blocked) report shape so the lead can route deterministically (Reviewer cites specific defect + canonical fix → lead dispatches request_maintenance carrying the fix).
3. Discriminator-level: 'Flat / Reviewer-cited correction' becomes an explicit pathway under request_maintenance, not just refactor/doc/value-update.

Also worth noting the substantive Implementer defect that triggered this: Implementer fabricated 4 @scenario_hash values rather than running scenarios.hash.compute_scenario_hash on the modified files. Reviewer caught it. §4.4 loop validation on a real Implementer error — the kind synthetic prototypes never produced.

Related: lead-231 (parent brief), lead-231.2 (the blocked dispatch), findings/from-prototype-1.md finding 3 / §8 (the prior 'documented but unexercised' note).

title: Catalog work_id pattern asymmetry — Clarify rejects what AssignScenarios/RequestBugfix accept
description:
Surfaced by Architect during brief-001 items A+C dispatch on 2026-05-12.

During dispatch of lead-231.1 (AssignScenarios) and lead-231.2 (RequestBugfix) to shopsystem-messaging, the Architect used dotted child bead IDs (lead-231.1, lead-231.2) as work_ids. Both schemas accepted them — neither AssignScenarios.work_id nor RequestBugfix.work_id carries a pattern constraint (just str). But Clarify.work_id has pattern '^[a-zA-Z0-9-]+$' which rejects dots.

This is a latent failure: if the BC sends Clarify on either lead-231.1 or lead-231.2, the BC-side 'shop-msg respond clarify --work-id lead-231.1' will fail schema validation. The protocol's symmetry across message types breaks at clarify-time.

What to do: align work_id patterns across catalog schemas. Three shapes worth considering — (a) tighten AssignScenarios/RequestBugfix/RequestMaintenance/WorkDone/MechanismObservation to share Clarify's pattern, constraining work_id naming uniformly; (b) loosen Clarify to match the others (just str), accepting more chaos but unblocking; (c) pick a shared canonical pattern (probably (a)'s shape with an explicit grammar) and apply uniformly with a single Pydantic type alias. Architect's call after pre-state verification.

Adjacent finding (do not scope here, just note): shop-msg has no recall command for in-flight dispatches, and the encapsulation invariant from lead-231 prohibits rm-ing inbox files. The two existing lead-231.1/.2 dispatches will tolerate this gap because they're more likely to round-trip via work_done than clarify, but recall-side encapsulation is a structural concern worth its own future brief.

Related: lead-231 (brief that surfaced this).

title: mechanism_observation: cross-tracker work_id pattern (shopsystem-templates-918 from shopsystem-bc-launcher)
description:
Observed during shopsystem-templates-918 clarify drain (2026-05-22, reconfirmed 2026-05-27): the work_id 'shopsystem-templates-918' is prefixed with one BC's tracker name (shopsystem-templates) while the actual sender of the assign_scenarios was a different BC (shopsystem-bc-launcher). 'bd search 918' returns no lead-side bead; the lead-gua bead's NOTES treat 918 as a BC-side tracker ID.

This is a cross-tracker work_id pattern: the on-wire work_id is the issuing BC's bead ID, not the lead's bead ID. §6 of the spec names the lead's bead ID as the canonical work_id, so this is an asymmetry worth pinning.

Related: lead-4wy (Catalog work_id pattern asymmetry — Clarify rejects what AssignScenarios/RequestBugfix accept). That bead documents catalog-schema validation asymmetry; this bead documents the *source-of-truth* asymmetry — which tracker's beads supply the work_id when the dispatch crosses BCs without going through the lead.

In this specific case the dispatch was actually lead-side (lead-gua) but the on-wire work_id 'shopsystem-templates-918' was set to the BC-side tracker reference. Whether that was an intentional cross-reference or a misuse is unclear from the surviving evidence.

A formal mechanism_observation cannot be emitted from the lead (the respond subcommands are BC-side). This bead is the lead-side equivalent record. If a future BC re-encounters the same pattern, it should emit a mechanism_observation through 'shop-msg respond mechanism_observation' citing this bead.

No immediate action required; observation only.

title: Future: shop-msg send clarify_response primitive (ADR-009 path a)
description:
Tracks the future implementation of a lead-side 'shop-msg send clarify_response --bc <name> --work-id <id> --resolution <text> [--scenario-file ...]' primitive per ADR-009 path (a).

Scope (coordinated change):
1. New ClarifyResponse Pydantic schema in repos/shopsystem-messaging/src/catalog/.
2. The lead-to-BC send path opts into allow_multi_type=True for clarify_response (storage.py:210-223) so the row can coexist with the original assign_scenarios/request_bugfix/request_maintenance row on the same work_id.
3. BC-side semantics decision (cf. lead-d6o design question #3): does receipt of clarify_response re-open the original dispatch for re-processing, or stand alone as a pinned answer?
4. shop-msg CLI surface: 'shop-msg send clarify_response' subcommand wired through send.py with --bc/--work-id/--resolution and optional --scenario-file repeats.
5. BC-side read path: 'shop-msg pending inbox' surfaces the clarify_response alongside the original dispatch.
6. Scenarios pinning (a) ClarifyResponse round-trips through the catalog schema, (b) lead-to-BC send with allow_multi_type writes both rows, (c) the BC reads both rows independently, (d) attempt to send clarify_response without prior clarify exit-codes appropriately (or is permissive — TBD).

Depends on:
- ADR-009 (this bead is the implementation target for the deferred path a).
- lead-d6o (multi-turn clarify semantics design).
- lead-8z1 (outbox uniqueness — adjacent: clarify rows in outbox).
- lead-4wy (catalog work_id pattern asymmetry).

Vehicle when implemented: assign_scenarios to shopsystem-messaging with PO-authored scenarios pending. PO authoring blocked on a coordinated brief revision that batches this with lead-d6o/lead-8z1/lead-rv9/lead-4wy per ADR-009 'Alternatives considered → Option A'.

Empirical pre-state (verified 2026-05-27):
- shop-msg send --help shows {request_maintenance, assign_scenarios, request_bugfix} only; no clarify_response.
- shop-msg respond clarify --help confirms BC-side direction (caller is the BC).
- storage.py:178,186-191 already carries allow_multi_type=True for BC-to-lead writes; the gate widening for lead-to-BC writes is the load-bearing change.

NOT blocking ADR-009's operational layer (b) — the de-facto re-dispatch pattern is canonical now and works without this primitive.
notes: Filed 2026-05-27 alongside ADR-009. Tracks the future implementation of 'shop-msg send clarify_response' primitive per ADR-009 path (a). Wired as blocked by lead-d6o, lead-8z1, lead-4wy — those design questions must resolve before this implementation target can be scoped. Operational use of clarify-resolution is canonized in ADR-009 path (b) and does not require this bead to ship.

The slugify path on the product field assumes a string; when the manifest contains a non-string YAML scalar (e.g. 'product: 123') the controller crashes with AttributeError rather than emitting a graceful 'non-string product' error message. title: shop-templates bootstrap fails on existing .beads/ — needs idempotent or skip-bd-init mode
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-13 setting up shopsystem-test-harness as a canonical shop.

shop-templates bootstrap shells out to 'bd init --skip-agents'. When the target dir already has an initialized .beads/, bd init errors with 'Found existing Dolt database... Aborting.' Bootstrap exits 1 with no scaffold written.

This blocks bootstrap on existing shops with a beads workspace — exactly the 'wrap existing repo' case brief 002 was scoped to handle.

Workaround today: 'shop-templates update' (doesn't shell to bd init) creates .claude/agents/ + canonical settings.json; CLAUDE.md re-rendered manually via python invocation of _render_claude_md() since update is init-only on CLAUDE.md per brief 002 item D.

Resolution shapes:
(a) Bootstrap detects existing .beads/ and skips bd init (idempotent). Matches brief 002's 'wrap existing repo' invariant.
(b) Bootstrap gains --skip-bd-init flag for explicit user control.
(c) Update gains optional CLAUDE.md re-render flag for the case of already-bd-init'd shops migrating to canonical.

(a) + (c) together cover the wrap-existing case cleanly. Worth a brief revision in the bootstrap area.

title: Framework convention: @supersedes:<hash> tag for scenario replacement in inter-shop messaging
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-13 during lead-5mz dispatch (brief 003 §4.4 closure for lead-xkh).

When a new scenario supersedes a previously-dispatched one, the relationship is currently expressed only in prose (Gherkin Feature description block, dispatch description). The Architect dispatching lead-5mz noted that adding an @supersedes:<hash> tag at dispatch time would make the relationship machine-readable in the BC's scenario register, rather than buried in prose.

Concrete shape: catalog schema's @bc:<name> and @scenario_hash:<hash> are existing tag conventions injected by shop-msg send. @supersedes:<hash> would be a parallel injection at dispatch time (could be a --supersedes flag on shop-msg send, or auto-detected from scenario body, or set via PO's authoring tag).

Benefits:
- Machine-readable retire-from-feature-file instruction for the BC's Implementer.
- Audit trail in the BC's outbox when work_done attests against new scenarios.
- Potentially enables a future 'scenario register' surface (lead-otu) to track supersede chains.

Tradeoffs:
- Introduces a new tag convention; requires shop-msg schema or send-side tooling change.
- The prose mechanism works today; this is an ergonomic / discipline improvement.

Worth designing alongside the round-4 consolidation brief (lead-6t3) — that brief will already touch scenario discipline broadly. Could fold.

Related: lead-6t3 (round-4 outlook consolidation), lead-otu (scenario register surface), lead-wgv (scenarios hash canonicalization).

title: shop-templates bootstrap/update missing --target raises TypeError (consistency pass adjacent to lead-1uo)
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-13 by lead-1uo PO + Architect during scenario 54 authoring/dispatch.

Two adjacent missing-required-arg gaps in shop-templates CLI, same shape as lead-1uo (which only covers --shop-name on bootstrap):

1. shop-templates bootstrap --shop-type bc --shop-name foo (missing --target)
   → TypeError at cli.py:242 (Path(args.target) with None).

2. shop-templates update --shop-type bc (missing --target)
   → TypeError at cli.py:292 (Path(args.target) with None).

Note: shop-templates update missing --shop-type already produces a clean diagnostic (_validate_shop_type catches it at lines 394-406). So this consistency pass is narrower than 'audit all subcommands' — only --target on bootstrap + update need tightening.

Vehicle: request_bugfix (same shape as lead-ijr currently in flight). Could fold with lead-ijr's fix if its Implementer's solution generalizes (uniform _validate-style missing-arg diagnostic across CLI); otherwise dispatch separately after lead-ijr round-trip surfaces the chosen pattern.

Defer dispatch until lead-ijr (the lead-1uo fix) returns work_done — then decide whether the Implementer's fix shape extends naturally or whether a separate dispatch is cleaner.

Related: lead-1uo (--shop-name parent), lead-ijr (in-flight fix dispatch).
close_reason: Consistency-pass closure. Both gaps tightened: (1) shop-templates bootstrap missing --target now exits 2 cleanly with stderr naming --target (scenario fe59a11a88a9ab60); (2) shop-templates update missing --target same shape (scenario 8fe363bd46cb766c). Dispatched as lead-79q request_bugfix; Implementer added _validate_target helper paralleling _validate_shop_name from lead-ijr. work_done complete from BC. With this closure, brief 002's bootstrap surface has all known missing-required-arg gaps pinned with clean-error discipline (--shop-type, --shop-name, --target across bootstrap+update). Reviewer flagged that empty-string and nonexistent-path cases for --target are out-of-scope and consistent with --shop-name precedent (same shape, not gaps).

title: shop-templates bootstrap raises TypeError when --shop-name omitted (should be argparse error or default-derived)
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-13 during brief-002 reconciliation by Architect. Empirical probe ran 'shop-templates bootstrap --shop-type bc --target .' (without --shop-name) and got TypeError (None passed to str.replace) instead of an argparse-style error.

Not a regression introduced by brief-002 work — pre-existing surface gap. Either:
(a) --shop-name should be argparse-required, raising a clean parse error.
(b) --shop-name should default-derive (e.g., from --target basename).

P3 — ergonomic, not blocking. Adjacent to brief-002's CLI surface but out of scope of its committed invariants. Surfaced during post-state probe; not part of any in-flight dispatch.

title: bc-implementer singleton-inbox presumption constrains lead-shop dispatch cadence
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-12 during brief-002 Architect pre-state verification.

The bc-implementer canonical template at repos/shopsystem-templates/.claude/agents/bc-implementer.md line 49 says verbatim 'There is exactly one file there.' The Implementer subagent's role contract presumes a singleton inbox. The bc-router CLAUDE.md does not enforce serialization either — its dispatch rule is 'any unprocessed inbox dispatches the Implementer' with no ordering guarantee.

Concrete consequence: the lead shop cannot dispatch a new message to a BC while another work_id is still in flight (still in the BC's inbox, not yet processed to outbox). Architect for brief-002 detected this and held dispatch because lead-bwu was still pending in the templates BC inbox.

Design question: should the protocol/template
- (a) explicitly enforce serial in-flight (one message at a time per BC; lead shop blocks on outbox before next send), formalizing the current presumption?
- (b) relax the singleton presumption (Implementer template handles N-message inbox; BC-router picks one to dispatch per turn)?
- (c) introduce a queue concept in shop-msg (lead can deposit multiple; BC processes in FIFO order; protocol exposes 'next' subcommand)?

(a) is the lowest-touch and matches current template behavior. (c) is the most flexible and may align with the brief-001 invariant (shop-msg encapsulates storage). (b) is a middle ground.

Related: lead-4wy (work_id pattern asymmetry), lead-rv9 (work_done(blocked) protocol gap) — same shape of latent protocol constraint surfaced by real dispatches.

Currently lead-bwu remains pending in templates BC inbox; brief-002 dispatch is held until it lands. This bead captures the structural concern, not the operational hold.

title: shop-msg not on default PATH — requires venv activation for cold dispatches
description:
Surfaced during lead-kq0 reconciliation on 2026-05-12.

shop-msg is installed at /workspaces/shopsystem-product/.venv/bin/shop-msg but the venv is not activated by default. Any agent dispatched cold (fresh subagent, new shell) must 'source .venv/bin/activate' first or invoke the absolute path; otherwise 'shop-msg ...' fails with command-not-found. The lead-architect's reconciliation pass hit this and worked around it.

What needs to be done: pick one of (a) ensure venv activation is automatic for any lead-shop session (devcontainer postCreate / shell rc), (b) install shop-msg outside the venv so it is on PATH unconditionally, (c) update subagent templates and CLAUDE.md to always invoke the absolute path or activate first. (c) is the lowest-touch / no install changes; (a) or (b) are more systemic. Choose during pickup.

title: Scenario register surface for templates BC (lead-architect activity currently degenerate)
description:
Surfaced during lead-kq0 reconciliation on 2026-05-12.

The post-lead-kq0 lead-architect template names 'Reconcile scenario registers against assigned work' as a §3.2 activity. No register surface exists under features/templates/ — only the raw .gherkin files (01-16). The activity is structurally degenerate for the templates BC until a register is introduced; the role names a responsibility the mechanism doesn't yet realize.

What needs to be done: decide between (a) introduce a per-BC scenario register surface in the lead shop carrying authored / dispatched / signed-off status (designed once for all BCs, not just templates), or (b) accept reconciliation against the templates BC as degenerate by design and document why. If (a), the design likely interacts with the lead-shop drain workflow noted as deferred in findings/from-mechanism-observation-v1.md §6.

value: bd uses CWD to locate .beads/, NOT a fixed repo path. When CWD drifts into a sibling repo (e.g. repos/shopsystem-templates/), bd operations hit THAT repo's bd, not the lead shop's bd — and you see different prefixes (shopsystem-templates-*) and different issue sets. Symptom this session (2026-05-22): I cd'd into repos/shopsystem-templates early on, never cd'd back, then spent multiple turns convinced sub-agents were 'confabulating' lead-* IDs that bd 'didn't have'. They weren't confabulating — they were in the right CWD (lead repo) and I was in the wrong one. Workaround: pin every bd/shop-msg/git invocation to /workspaces/shopsystem-product as CWD (use cd or git -C), and treat 'bd show X says no issue found' as a CWD-check trigger before assuming the issue doesn't exist.

PyYAML 1.1 coerces GitHub Actions 'on' key to boolean True. In step defs parsing workflow YAML with yaml.safe_load(), use wf.get(True) not wf.get('on'). The shopsystem-devcontainer BC uses a _get_workflow_on() helper (tests/conftest.py:820) as the fix. Consider ruamel.yaml (YAML 1.2) to avoid this permanently.

shopsystem-bc-launcher: assert_docker_run_includes_flag step does not assert container_name against the run_cmd — latent fragility if multiple containers run in same test. BC noted this in lead-0eu work_done summary. Queue a request_bugfix once lead-90z and lead-cyo are processed.
